description_long: This object is a vintage film projector, featuring a compact, rectangular body with rounded edges and a light gray finish. It is mounted on a concrete slab, which is set within a bordered, pebbled surface, suggesting it is displayed as a public installation or monument. The projector has two prominent cylindrical lenses protruding from the front, with metallic finishes and adjustment rings, and a visible winding handle on the side. There are several small control dials and switches on the top and side, as well as a visible logo and labeling on the main body. A dark strap or cable is attached to the side, draping down toward the base. The overall design is utilitarian, with a focus on mechanical and optical components, and the object appears to be constructed primarily from metal and plastic.
description: A vintage, light gray film projector with dual metallic lenses, mounted on a concrete slab and displayed on a pebbled surface. The body features control dials, a winding handle, and a visible logo.
description_view_0: Front-left view showing both metallic lenses, the winding handle, and the main logo on the side.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the dual lenses and the control dials on the top and side.
description_view_2: Rear-left view displaying the side panel, strap or cable, and the back of the projector body.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the back panel and the attachment of the strap or cable.
attribute_brand_logo: A circular logo with the letters 'K' and 'Kodak' is visible on the left side of the projector body, featuring a red accent and classic font style.
attribute_lens_type: fixed dual lens with metallic adjustment rings, likely for different focal lengths.
attribute_control_panel_design: The control panel consists of several mechanical dials and switches located on the top and side of the projector, with clear labeling and tactile knobs.
attribute_ventilation_pattern: No visible ventilation pattern or grills are present on the exterior surfaces.
